(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue ware fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}}

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue ware fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}}

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue ware fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Images]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]]

Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue ware fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alias = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 37 | latitude_sec = 0.0 | longitude_deg = 134 | longitude_min = 9 | longitude_sec = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | associated_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | label = Hakayama Kofun | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alias = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 37 | latitude_sec = 0.0 | longitude_deg = 134 | longitude_min = 9 | longitude_sec = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | associated_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_cultural_property = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | label = Hakayama Kofun | label_position = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial facilities include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : Dimensions: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial Facility include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. Burial Facility include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:bump]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == Burial Facilities == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | label-pos1 = bottom | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2 = Kuroshima | label-pos2 = top | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3 = Kahoyama | label-pos3 = bottom | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4 = (Hakayama) | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5 = Futazukayama | label-pos5 = right | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]]
