(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[en:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. (Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year }}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year }}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}}

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}}

Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}}

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]
