{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shrank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 波歌山古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 =[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 0.0 |経度度 = 134|経度分 = 9|経度秒 = 14.0 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長60m
高さ8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[竪穴式石室]]1基
前方部：竪穴式石室1基 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル = 波歌山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在した丘陵跡
（中央の空閑地）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。左上の黒島に[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]、右の2丘陵に[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]が所在する。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.png | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.png | mark-size5=25 }} '''波歌山古墳'''（はかやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]]にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||[[牛窓天神山古墳]]||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||[[鹿歩山古墳]]||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||'''波歌山古墳'''||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓湾に面して綾浦・紺浦を隔てる丘陵（波歌山、標高約40メートル）上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[牛窓天神山古墳]]・[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られる。本古墳では[[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）に岡山県教育委員会による測量調査が実施されたのち、[[1969年]]（昭和44年）の土取り工事で墳丘が削平されており、現在は丘陵の一部のみを残存する{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南西方向に向けた{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=66}}。墳丘は2段築成と見られる{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=157-173}}{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘外表で[[葺石]]の存在は明らかでないが、[[円筒埴輪]]片・[[形象埴輪]]片（盾形埴輪か）が採集された{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は後円部・前方部における[[竪穴式石室]]各1基{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=66}}。またそれとは別に前方部南東部で木棺直葬と推測される施設が認められていた{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。特に吉備地方中枢部では同時期に古墳規模が大きく縮小するのに対して、牛窓では縮小度は小さい点が注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=129-132}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=157-173}}。 * 墳丘長：約60メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：約30メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 長さ：約34メートル ** 幅：約23-35メートル ** 高さ：約6メートル == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、後円部・前方部において各1基の[[竪穴式石室]]の構築が認められている{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=66}}。各石室の詳細は次の通り。 ; 後円部石室 : 石室規模は、長さ5.1メートル・幅1.2メートル・高さ2.1メートル{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=66}}。 : 石室主軸は墳丘主軸と平行方向{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。割石積みにより構築される{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 ; 前方部石室 : 石室規模は、長さ6メートル・幅1.3メートル・高さ1.2メートル{{Sfn|牛窓町古墳図|2002|p=66}}。 : 石室主軸は墳丘主軸と平行方向{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 以上のほか、前方部南東部で木棺直葬（推定）1基が確認されている{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。この木棺は長さ2メートル・幅0.7-0.8メートルを測り、下に角礫を敷いており、副葬品としてガラス小玉3・甲冑片・鉄鏃片・須恵器片が検出されている{{Sfn|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=波歌山古墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=157-173|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=波歌山古墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図|publisher=牛窓町教育委員会|isbn=|page=66|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=波歌山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|波歌山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=波歌山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|波歌山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=波歌山古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はかやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

高さ8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 後円部：[[竪穴式石室]]1基
前方部：竪穴式石室1基 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南西 |ラベル = 波歌山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在した丘陵跡
（中央の空閑地）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。左上の黒島に[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]、右の2丘陵に[[鹿歩山古墳]]・[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]が所在する。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.png | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.png | mark-size5=25 }} '''波歌山古墳'''（はかやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[牛窓|牛窓町牛窓]]にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||[[牛窓天神山古墳]]||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||[[鹿歩山古墳]]||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||'''波歌山古墳'''||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 37 | coordinates = 0.0 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 9 | coordinates = 14.0 | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} x1

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}}

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|qq=draft}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft}} {{citation needed|Namikayama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Namikayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]]

Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[en:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]]
