(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja|昭和43年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}, but by {{ill|1969|ja|昭和44年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|ja||1968年|1=1968|2=ja|3=1968年|lt=Showa 43|lt_ja=昭和43年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1968年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but by {{ill|1969|ja||1969年|1=1969|2=ja|3=1969年|lt=Showa 44|lt_ja=昭和44年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1969年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Namikayama Kofun]] [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futazukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}}

The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}}

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}}

Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|qq=|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}}

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}}
