To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}}

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in [[:en:1968|Showa 43]], but by [[:en:1969|Showa 44]], the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], marking the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hakayama Kofun | image = | alternate_name = | affiliation = | location = {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.616667|N|134.153889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso = JP-33 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length: 60 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=Showa 43}}, but by {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=Showa 44}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=Showa 43}}, but by {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=Showa 44}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Height: 8 m (rear mound) | burial_facilities = Rear mound: {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} x1
Front mound: Vertical stone chamber x1 | burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments | associated_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | Interred_individual = | mausoleum = | historic_site = None | tangible_notes = | remarks = Mound no longer exists | map = Japan Okayama | type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest | map_dot_label = Hakayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The hill where the tomb was located
(Central open space)}}{{small|Viewed from the {{ill|Ushimado Olive Garden|ja|牛窓オリーブ園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Olive Garden|qq=}}. To the upper left is {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; to the right are {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}]] {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of keyhole-shaped Kofuns in Ushimado Bay}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Ushimado Tenjinyama | map_dot_label-pos1 = bottom | map_dot_label-color1 = black | mark1 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size2 = 25 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kahoyama | map_dot_label-pos3 = bottom | map_dot_label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Northeast.png | mark-size3 = 25 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | map_dot_label4 = (Hakayama) | map_dot_label-pos4 = bottom | map_dot_label-color4 = black | mark4 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southwest.png | mark-size4 = 25 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Futazukayama | map_dot_label-pos5 = right | map_dot_label-color5 = black | mark5 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=Showa 43|1=1968}}, but by {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=Showa 44|1=1969}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

South-southeast.png | mark-size1 = 25 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kuroshima | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | map_dot_label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - Southeast.png | mark-size5 = 25 }} '''Hakayama Kofun''' (波歌山古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|ja|牛窓町牛窓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture|1=Ushimado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570212}}. The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=Showa 43|1=1968}}, but by {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=Showa 44|1=1969}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:波歌山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55523153}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]

The mound no longer exists. == Overview == Hakayama Kofun was constructed atop a hill (Hakayama, elevation approximately 40 meters) overlooking Ushimado Bay in the southeastern part of Okayama Prefecture. This tomb is one of five keyhole-shaped mounds distributed around Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}., {{ill|Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kabuyama Kofun|lt=Kahoyama Kofun|ja|鹿歩山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kahoyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. The Okayama Prefectural Board of Education conducted a survey in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=Showa 43|1=1968}}, but by {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=Showa 44|1=1969}}, the mound had been leveled during excavation work, leaving only part of the hill. The tomb's mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented southwest. The mound likely had two tiers. While the presence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} has not been confirmed, fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and shield-shaped haniwa have been collected. burial include a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections, as well as a presumed wooden coffin burial in the southeastern part of the front section. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, marking the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Along with the other four keyhole-shaped mounds, Hakayama Kofun highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port in that era. Notably, while tombs in central Kibi shRank significantly in size during this period, those in Ushimado exhibited only minimal size reduction. == Mound area == The mound's area are as follows: * Mound length: Approximately 60 meters * Rear mound: ** Diameter: Approximately 30 meters ** Height: Approximately 8 meters ** Two-tier construction * Front mound: ** Length: Approximately 34 meters ** Width: Approximately 23–35 meters ** Height: Approximately 6 meters ** Two-tier construction == burial == The tomb contained a vertical stone chamber in both the rear and front sections: ; Rear Stone Chamber : area: Length 5.1 meters, Width 1.2 meters, Height 2.1 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. ; Front Stone Chamber : area: Length 6 meters, Width 1.3 meters, Height 1.2 meters. : The chamber axis aligns with the main axis of the mound. Additionally, a presumed wooden coffin burial was identified in the southeastern part of the front section. Measuring 2 meters in length and 0.7–0.8 meters in width, the coffin was placed on a bed of angular gravel and contained grave goods, including three glass beads, fragments of armor, iron arrowheads, and Sue pottery fragments. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|波歌山古墳|version=95137541|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q55523153|ja|波歌山古墳}} [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
